name: Baseline-006: char-level TF-IDF + linear classifier
description: Char n-gram TF-IDF (analyzer=char_wb, ngram_range=(3,5)) + balanced one-vs-rest LR. Identical training corpus + classifier head to Baseline-005; only the vectorizer's analyzer differs. Tests whether sub-word lexical features pick up the folklore signature where word-level cannot.
pre_stated_prediction: submissions/baseline_006_tfidf_char/PRE_STATED_PREDICTION.md committed BEFORE gauntlet run, at commit 19bdb8c. Predicted: K3 < 0.30 AND recall < 0.17 = n=2 confirmation. Actual: K3 = 0.222, recall = 0.067 — falls cleanly in the n=2 confirmation region.
notes: Exact-metric tie with Baseline-005 (word-level TF-IDF): same K1 0.30, same K2 0.74, same K3 0.22, same recall 0.067. This goes beyond 'char-level loses to embeddings' — char-level produces an IDENTICAL prediction pattern to word-level on this benchmark. n=2 evidence: classical bag-of-tokens cannot pick up the dark-core signature at any lexical granularity. The signal lives in semantic-similarity space, not in surface-form n-grams of any width.
